sn2.12:1.1: At Sāvatthī. Standing to one side, the young deva Veṇhu recited this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:[n.162] I adopt Se and Ee2 Veṇhu over Be and Ee1 Veṇḍu; the reading Veṇṇu in SS may, however, be the historical form. The name is the Pāli equivalent of Skt Viṣṇu; perhaps this young deva is a prototype of the Hindu deity.
